PHASE: Phase 7 active — fourteenth Feature Update merged. Phase 7 #14 (in-call-state) squash-merged 2026-05-18 as `6d3b6f8`. Adds the yellow `in_call` dot on the Speed Dial Board: a twin-roster + twin-hook engine parallel to Phase 7 #11's presence engine. Browser emits `call:joined`/`call:left` on LiveKit `Room.Connected`/`Room.Disconnected` via a new composable `useEmitCallParticipation(room)` hook invoked by both `useLiveKitRoom` and `useMeetingRoom`. Server tracks per-org per-user socket-set roster with `{wasFirst}/{isLast}` coalescing in `createInCallRoster()`; broadcasts `call:active` org-scoped via `emitToOrg` on transitions only; sends `call:active-snapshot` socket-direct on connect. `selectDepartmentPresence(dept, online, inCall)` overlays in_call > online > offline. UI (yellow dot + disabled state in `<SpeedDialButton>`) was already wired since Phase 4 Part 5b — this ticket added the missing data plane. Closes second deferred-scope from Phase 7 #12.

TIER_CLASSIFICATION: 2 — moderate. Per memory-governance §1: 13 implementation files across 4 modules with dependency depth 2. Score = (13 × 2.5) + (4 × 5) + (2 × 3) = 58.5 — comfortably below the Tier 3 threshold (80) and well within the 80K SAFE zone. Single-session inline execution successful.

PHASE_7_TICKET_14_NOTES:
  - Why client-emit (not LiveKit webhooks): LIVEKIT_WEBHOOK_KEY is ⏳ in CREDENTIALS.md; webhook path adds an external surface for marginal benefit when the client-emit attack surface is "attacker marks self in_call → blocks own calls → hurts only attacker". Server sources userId from socket.data.session (Phase 7 #8e auth middleware), so client cannot lie about identity.
  - Why twin-roster (not extending presence): coupling two independent state machines (socket-count vs LiveKit-room-membership) into one Record return type breaks useUserPresence's existing signature for callers that don't need in_call. Separate hooks compose cleanly via selectDepartmentPresence's 3rd param.
  - Why in_call > online > offline precedence: in_call is the authoritative signal during a live call. Edge case (in_call set still has user but online map already cleared) returns "in_call" anyway — brief window before transport timeout cleans up the roster is acceptable. Locked decision 3.
  - Why useEmitCallParticipation as separate file (refinement vs spec): spec sketched inline edits in /app/call/[id]/page.tsx, but actual Room lifecycle owners are useLiveKitRoom (intercom) and useMeetingRoom (scheduled meetings). A composable hook invoked from BOTH avoids duplication and covers the meeting flow too. Documented in plan as deviation.
  - Why no defense-in-depth at calls.end: calls.end fires from caller's RoomEvent.Disconnected handler — the same browser also fires call:left from useEmitCallParticipation in parallel. Adding redundant in_call clear at calls.end would be a no-op safety net for the implausible "browser survives long enough to call tRPC but fails to emit a single socket event" scenario. Clean separation: calls.end = audit; socket events = realtime state. Locked decision 7.
  - Why hook test deferred for useUsersInCall + useEmitCallParticipation: no jsdom infra in project (same trade-off as Phase 7 #11's use-user-presence). The pure handler tests in in-call-handler.test.ts cover the data-flow contract; the hooks are thin React glue (useState + useEffect + useSocketOptional consumer). Visual QA smoke test deferred to Rule 16 follow-up.
  - Why inline (not subagent) execution: User explicitly chose inline given Phase 7 #13's Sonnet thrash on Task 3 (which had similar shape — read 2 large files + write impl + commit in one dispatch). Inline kept controller's Opus 4.7 context well-managed throughout (~55K total).
